info.title: AgentMemory API
info.description:
AgentMemory is a persistent, semantic memory service for AI agents. It stores conversation
history, extracted facts, and vector embeddings, enabling agents to recall relevant past
context across sessions and conversations.

## Core concepts

AgentMemory organizes data in a three-level hierarchy:

- **User** — a persistent identity (human end-user, agent instance, or service account)
  that owns one or more sessions. A user must exist before sessions or memory can be created.
- **Session** — a scoped conversation context owned by a user. The session is the default
  boundary for semantic search — results are drawn from the current session unless the
  request explicitly expands scope. Sessions can be ended to prevent further writes.
- **Memory block** — the atomic unit of stored knowledge. Each block holds either a
  **chat message** (a user-turn and assistant-turn exchange) or a **fact** (a standalone
  declarative string). Blocks are independently addressable and retrievable.

## Semantic extraction

When a memory block is written, AgentMemory automatically generates a vector embedding and an
optional LLM-generated summary. By default this happens asynchronously in the background.
Blocks are immediately readable after ingestion but only participate in semantic search once
their `status` reaches `ready`. Blocks with `status: processing` or `status: extraction_failed`
are excluded from search results.

## Authentication

Authentication is optional and controlled by the `OIDC_AUTH_ENABLED` server configuration.
When enabled, all endpoints except `GET /health` and `GET /metrics` require a valid JWT
Bearer token issued by the configured OIDC provider.

Include the token in every request:
```
Authorization: Bearer <token>
```

Tokens are validated against the provider's JWKS endpoint. A `401` response indicates a
missing, malformed, or expired token. A `403` response indicates a valid token with
insufficient permissions.

## Request and response format

All request and response bodies use `application/json`. All timestamps are ISO 8601 strings
in UTC. Errors are returned as a JSON object with an `error` code, a human-readable
`message`, and an optional `details` field.

## Rate limiting and ingestion

Memory block ingestion is accepted immediately, but semantic extraction (embedding generation
and summarization) is rate-limited by the configured model provider. If the extraction queue
reaches capacity, ingestion requests return `503` with a `retry_after_seconds` field.

get.description: Return server health status, version, and uptime. Public endpoint — no authentication required.
Verify that AgentMemory can reach and query the Couchbase database.
Check reachability and status of the configured embedding and LLM model services.
Return lightweight readiness status for the semantic extraction queue.
Return detailed queue depth, model API rate budget, and cumulative throughput statistics for the semantic extraction queue.
Return current memory usage relative to the configured quota threshold. When usage exceeds the threshold, new ingestion requests are rejected until pressure subsides.
Retrieve all users. Returns an empty list if no users exist.

post.description:
Create a new user with the specified ID, name, and optional metadata.
The `user_id` must be unique — attempting to create a user with an existing ID returns a conflict error.

Find users matching the provided criteria (`user_id`, `name`, or `metadata`).
Multiple criteria are combined with AND logic. At least one criterion must be provided.

schema.title: Response Search Users Users Search Post

put.description:
Update an existing user's `name` and/or `metadata`.
At least one field must be provided. Omitted fields retain their existing values.

delete.description:
Permanently delete a user and all associated sessions and memory blocks.
This operation is irreversible — there is no soft-delete or recovery.

put.description:
Update the time-to-live (TTL) for memory blocks belonging to a user.
Optionally scope the update to specific sessions or specific block IDs.

get.description: Retrieve all sessions for a user, including lifecycle state and annotations. Returns an empty list if the user has no sessions.

post.description:
Create a new session for the specified user.
The `session_id` must be unique per user — attempting to create a session with a duplicate ID returns a conflict error.

get.description: Retrieve a session by ID, including its lifecycle state, annotations, and metadata.

put.description:
Update a session's annotations and/or metadata.
At least one field must be provided. Omitted fields retain their existing values.

delete.description: Permanently delete a session and all its memory blocks. This operation is irreversible.

post.description:
Mark a session as ended. Once ended, no new memory blocks can be added.
Existing memory blocks remain readable and searchable. Returns the updated session with `end_time` set.

Add one or more memory blocks to the session. Each block holds either a chat message
(user + assistant turn) or a fact (declarative string). Blocks are written immediately
and queued for semantic extraction. The session must be open — ended sessions reject new blocks.

delete.description:
Delete memory blocks by ID. Pass a list of block IDs to delete specific blocks,
or `"all"` to delete every block in the session.

put.description:
Update the content, annotations, or TTL of an existing memory block.
Providing a new message or fact triggers re-extraction (new embedding and summary).
Omitted fields retain their existing values. Use this endpoint to retry extraction
on blocks with `status: extraction_failed` by setting `async_processing: true`.
If the block does not exist or has expired due to TTL, responds with 404.

parameters.name: block_id

schema.description: Unique identifier for the memory block
schema.title: Block Id

parameters.description: Unique identifier for the memory block

post.description:
Retrieve memory blocks using semantic similarity and/or filters. Provide a natural-language
`query` to rank blocks by relevance, or use `filters` alone for deterministic retrieval.
Search is session-scoped by default — set `filters.session_ids` to `"all"` to search
across all sessions for the user. Only `ready` blocks appear in results.

get.description:
Paginated list of memory blocks for a user, ordered newest first.
Use `session_ids` to scope results to specific sessions.
Always specify `limit` and `offset` — unbounded requests on large datasets are slow.

parameters.name: session_ids

schema.description: Comma-separated session IDs to filter by, or 'all' for all sessions
schema.title: Session Ids

parameters.description: Comma-separated session IDs to filter by, or 'all' for all sessions

parameters.name: limit

schema.description: Maximum number of memory blocks to return (1–200)
schema.title: Limit

parameters.description: Maximum number of memory blocks to return (1–200)

parameters.name: offset

schema.description: Number of memory blocks to skip for pagination
schema.title: Offset

parameters.description: Number of memory blocks to skip for pagination

parameters.name: order_by

schema.description: Field to order results by. Defaults to 'ingested_at'.
schema.title: Order By

parameters.description: Field to order results by. Defaults to 'ingested_at'.

get.description:
Download server logs and optional system diagnostics as a ZIP archive.
Use `log_types` to select log categories and `start_time`/`end_time` to narrow the time range.
Add `sys_commands` to include live system snapshots (CPU, memory, disk, network) in the archive.
Include this archive in support requests and incident post-mortems.

parameters.name: start_time

schema.description: Include log lines at or after this timestamp
schema.title: Start Time

parameters.description: Include log lines at or after this timestamp

parameters.name: end_time

schema.description: Include log lines at or before this timestamp
schema.title: End Time

parameters.description: Include log lines at or before this timestamp

parameters.name: sys_commands

schema.description: Optional system commands to run and include in the archive
schema.title: Sys Commands

parameters.description: Optional system commands to run and include in the archive

example.message: Invalid sys_commands: foo. Supported values: sys_info, uptime, disk, diskstats, processes, network, meminfo, vmstat, os_release, net_dev

created_at.title: Created At
created_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp indicating when the data was originally created. Stored as null if not provided.

examples.reason: memory block exceeds per-request token limit

AsyncBatchProcessorHealthResponse.title: AsyncBatchProcessorHealthResponse
AsyncBatchProcessorHealthResponse.description: Aggregated health payload for the async batch processor endpoint.

AsyncBatchQueueStats.title: AsyncBatchQueueStats
AsyncBatchQueueStats.description: Queue depth and processing counts for async batch processor.

AsyncBatchRateBudget.title: AsyncBatchRateBudget
AsyncBatchRateBudget.description: Snapshot of remaining request/token capacity and configured limits.

AsyncBatchStatistics.title: AsyncBatchStatistics
AsyncBatchStatistics.description: Cumulative async batch processor counters for observability.

start_time.title: Start Time
start_time.description: Inclusive lower bound on ingested_at (when the block was stored). For semantic search this is a pre-filter inside the FTS KNN index, so only blocks ingested within [start_time, end_time) enter the candidate pool.

end_time.title: End Time
end_time.description: Exclusive upper bound on ingested_at. See start_time.

created_start_time.title: Created Start Time
created_start_time.description: Inclusive lower bound on created_at (the original data-creation timestamp, which may differ from ingestion time for historic imports). For semantic search this is also a pre-filter inside the FTS KNN index. When both ingested_at and created_at ranges are provided they are ANDed: a block must satisfy both ranges to be returned.

created_end_time.title: Created End Time
created_end_time.description: Exclusive upper bound on created_at. See created_start_time.

session_ids.title: Session Ids
session_ids.description: List of session IDs to search, 'all' for all sessions, or None for current session only

order_by.title: Order By
order_by.description: Field to order results by. Defaults to 'ingested_at'.

count.title: Count
count.description: Number of memory blocks returned in this response

total.title: Total
total.description: Total number of memory blocks matching the query scope

limit.title: Limit
limit.description: Maximum number of memory blocks requested (1-200)

ListMemoriesResponse.title: ListMemoriesResponse
ListMemoriesResponse.description: Response for listing memory blocks with pagination.

ModelHealth.title: ModelHealth
ModelHealth.description: Health info for a single model service.

SessionListResponse.title: SessionListResponse
SessionListResponse.description: Response wrapper for list of sessions.

annotations.title: Annotations
annotations.description: New annotations to overwrite existing ones. If None, existing annotations are preserved.

memory_block_ttl.title: Memory Block Ttl
memory_block_ttl.description: New TTL in seconds. If None, existing TTL is preserved.

async_processing.title: Async Processing
async_processing.description: If True, semantic extraction runs in background via queue.

context_required.title: Context Required
context_required.description: Whether semantic extraction is required. If None, uses environment variable.

block.description: The updated memory block. Always populated on a 200 response; missing or expired blocks return 404 instead.

UserListResponse.title: UserListResponse
UserListResponse.description: Response wrapper for list of users.

tags.name: Users
tags.description:
Create and manage user identities. A **user** is the top-level entity in the AgentMemory hierarchy — every session and memory block is owned by a user.

The `user_id` is application-defined and must be unique across the deployment. Use your application's native user identifier (UUID, account ID, or similar) as `user_id` to avoid maintaining a separate mapping table.

Deleting a user is a **cascade operation** — it permanently removes all sessions and memory blocks associated with that user. There is no soft-delete or recovery.

tags.name: Sessions
tags.description:
Create and manage conversation sessions. A **session** scopes memory blocks and defines the default retrieval boundary for semantic search.

Sessions have a two-state lifecycle:
- **Open** (default) — memory can be added, updated, searched, and deleted.
- **Ended** — the session is read-only; no new memory blocks can be added. Call `POST /users/{user_id}/sessions/{session_id}/end` to end a session.

Deleting a session cascades to all of its memory blocks.

tags.name: Memory
tags.description:
Add, retrieve, update, and search memory blocks. A **memory block** is the atomic unit of knowledge — each block holds either a chat message exchange or a standalone fact, and is automatically processed for semantic search.

**Adding memory** — Submit messages or facts in a single request. Semantic extraction runs asynchronously by default; blocks are readable immediately and become searchable once extraction completes.

**Searching memory** — Provide a natural-language query to retrieve semantically similar blocks. Search is session-scoped by default. Set `filters.session_ids` to `"all"` to search across all sessions for a user. Only `ready` blocks are returned.

**TTL and expiry** — Memory blocks carry an optional time-to-live in seconds. TTL can be set per-block, per-session, or globally via server configuration.

tags.name: Health
tags.description:
Monitor the operational status of AgentMemory and its dependencies.

`GET /health` is a public endpoint suitable for load balancer health probes. All other health endpoints require authentication and provide deeper diagnostic detail about the database connection, model service availability, and the semantic extraction queue.

| Status | Meaning |
|--------|---------|
| `healthy` | Component is operating normally |
| `degraded` | Component is reachable but not fully functional |
| `unhealthy` | Component is unreachable or critically impaired |

tags.name: Metrics
tags.description: Expose Prometheus-compatible metrics for monitoring and alerting. This endpoint is public and requires no authentication, making it suitable for Prometheus scrape targets and infrastructure monitoring tools.

tags.name: Logs
tags.description: Download a ZIP archive of server logs and optional system diagnostics. Use this endpoint to collect diagnostic data for support requests or incident post-mortems. Requires authentication.

host.description: Hostname and port of the AgentMemory deployment
